subject: Re: Admin Fee Data Discovery
bodyPreview: Bill, this is helpful context. I can see what Joe is trying to get after as part of this Admin Fee project, and there is a decent amount of upstream data overlap with the Portfolio Expansion (PE) project. Do you want us to start to step in to help support
bodyText:
Bill, this is helpful context. I can see what Joe is trying to get after as part of this Admin Fee project, and there is a decent amount of upstream data overlap with the Portfolio Expansion (PE) project. Do you want us to start to step in to help support Joe’s asks below?

If we need to start right away, I will tap the Fusion Team working on the PE project, but eventually we may spin out a second Fusion Team - keeping one embedded with the PE work stream and embedding a second in the Admin Fee workstream. That way each could have their own "product owner” from the BU but they would share a data model/layer through ABI’s platform (“hub”). When it comes time to loop these initial analytics together into an app, we can pick it up within the core ABI team.

With all the work driven by Bruce’s vision & the Value Creation Initiatives, I’m thinking of creating a sourcing/GPO focused lane with ABI. Currently we have Provider, Supplier, Platform, and CoC lanes. With the addition of Jonathan Pruitt’s team, we now have a 5th sub-team. They seemed consumed by the new Negotiate platform (legacy Conductive tech in R&M mode?), so perhaps we build out this team to become a GPO-centric lane. It would be great if someday these lagging (admin fee, coverage) and leading (market share movement, contract value) indicators of GPO health might be visible right within the core contracting app.

​A couple concerns related to the Admin Fee & PE projects: 
Who should be the decider for conflicts between the two? e.g. prioritization of work for shared resources, conflicting metric definitions, etc.
If we spend too much time measuring leaders on admin fee $ generated and $ on contract, we may fall back into expanding the portfolio without strengthening the contract. I planned to schedule time with Joe this week to share our analysis around how effective the performance groups are at moving market share (vs. National), and Stephen has some interesting analysis of the trends in locally contracted volumes. Is Joe the best person to share this with, at least for Med-Surg, and if so is the timing appropriate?

Matt

From: Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>
Date: Monday, February 23, 2026 at 3:51 PM
To: Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>, Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>
Subject: FW: Admin Fee Data Discovery

 

 

From: Bichler, Joe <Joe_Bichler@premierinc.com>
Sent: Monday, February 23, 2026 1:26 AM
To: Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>
Cc: Rollins, Rachael <Rachael_Rollins@PremierInc.com>; Chrystal, Danielle <Danielle_Chrystal@premierinc.com>; Bichler, Joe <Joe_Bichler@premierinc.com>
Subject: RE: Admin Fee Data Discovery

 

Evening Bill,

 

I will spend a bit more time this week with Rachael to scope out the timeline and data request requirements. For now and to help start the data kick off process, lets think about using the attached to do a quick data scrub collection.

 

*** Timeline Goal for this first draft Raw Data Collection: March 6th

 

*** The attached was shared with us as what we report out and should be a conclusive list.  If we can add the following into the attached report:

Supplier Top Parent Cross Reference  I assume we have this easily and can populate the attached with a top parent rollup 

Service Line/Program Line Rollup Cross ReferenceDo you have a cross reference for Program Line (Clinical, Non-Clinical, Food, Pharmacy)? If not, then let us know and we will build one.Do you have a cross reference for Service Line (Surgical, Nursing, PPI, Facilities, etc.) If not, then let us know and we will build one. 

Commitment Cross ReferenceDo you have a cross reference to look at the contract # nomenclature and specify if this is SURPASS, AD, Intersectta, etc? If not, then we will build one. 

Sales Volume Per Contract for Calendar Year 2025  (per each row) 

Gross Admin Fee Per Contract for Calendar Year 2025 (per each row) 

Calculated column for the WAAF % (per each row) 

***Revenue Ask

Since the above was all based on contracts admin fee, can you locate other revenue from suppliers like MSA’s, Market Insights, Pharmacies 2% they keep, etc. If not, let me know and I will locate a resource in finance and talk to the core group. 

 

 

 

From: Bichler, Joe <Joe_Bichler@premierinc.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 17, 2026 5:07 PM
To: Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>
Cc: Rollins, Rachael <Rachael_Rollins@PremierInc.com>; Chrystal, Danielle <Danielle_Chrystal@premierinc.com>
Subject: RE: Admin Fee Data Discovery

 

Bill,

 

Thanks for the collaboration today.

 

As a follow-up for the data collection aspect of the admin fee rate, below are some thoughts generated from this exercise about data gathering phase. Would value you, your teams, etc insights on if there are components to be a value add if there are gaps as well.

 

 

 

Current State Data Collection Exercise

Current Goal/Purpose: Baseline measurement of each Service Line, Program Offering, Supplier, Teammate, Leader rolled up into a drillable report based on the below requirements. 

Future Goal : We will need to be ability to have this report be an interactive dashboard that we can pull up and filter/drill where needed. The ability to have it be refreshed on a predetermined cycle (i.e. nightly) to update. 

 

Requirements / The Ask. Thinking about having 4 different views of a similar baseline data report that have similar ingredients. Just rolling it up into different views. You have offered strong advice on how to look at this, so truly open to alternative solutions here but intent is to get a baseline to get the leaders to agree on a baseline / day 0. 

Service Line Report at the header level by each service line.  Ex. Pharmacy, Food, Clinical, Non-Clinical, and Technology.                                                              i.      Additionally, request to be drillable into sub service line. Ex. Clinical drills to Surgical. Non-Clinical can be drilled into Construction, etc.

                                                            ii.      Additionally, want to creatively drill then here by supplier as well. We probably need to mock it up together to see what works.  

                                                          iii.      Data Points included in the report: sliced by sales volume, by gross admin fee (GAF), by weighted average % (WAAF)), …. Then also by columns of other revenue. To your point, can we create some columns of alternative review like MSA’s, Market Insights, etc….. Also want to include the supplier names  

                                                           iv.      Historical: Would like to see if we can slice it by month, quarter, fiscal year … and include some sort of trending component here dating back… if possible… 5 years?

 

Programmatic View: Would see if we can roll up a report by programmatic offering like SURPASS, AscenDrive, and National / Other program offerings by division.                                                              i.      Same as above. Service Lines if we can and sub service line. Data points are similar and the historical.

 

Supplier Report Report: Looking for a separate report here that you can toggle between to see by supplier overall rather than division of premier.                                                              i.      Data Points: To your point, want to think about sales volume, by gross admin fee (GAF), by weighted average % (WAAF)) but also alternative revenue like MSA’s, market insights, etc.

                                                            ii.      Historical: Would like to see if we can slice it by month, quarter, fiscal year … and include some sort of trending component here dating back… if possible… 5 years?

 

Team Member / Leader Report : Looking for a report here that we can identify by team member what the admin fee they have generated.                                                              i.      Data Points: Interested to pull down how many contracts they wrote, what was the admin fee average rate, the volume projected or actual off that contract /admin fee. Probably some of the same indications aboe around Sales volume, GAF, WAAF, MSA’s, Market Insights, etc.

                                                            ii.      Data: would also like to see if we can try to line them up to the service line, sub service line

                                                          iii.      Data: wonder if we can toss in if they contract for a program like SURPASS

                                                           iv.      Historical: Would like to see if we can slice it by the same. Month, quarter, fiscal year, etc. dating back a few years if possible.

 

 

 

Requirements: Team member contract KPI's. How many contracts they wrote, what admin fee was associated, and the volume (projected & actual) off that contract. Dating back 5 years ago with rolling data going forward ability to see trending and filters by month, quarter, Fiscal year. Ability to see by service line they belong to and sub service line as well as if they belong to a program like SURPASS. 

 

 

 

 

 

-----Original Appointment-----
From: Bichler, Joe
Sent: Monday, February 2, 2026 5:01 PM
To: Bichler, Joe; Marquardt, Bill
Cc: Rollins, Rachael; Chrystal, Danielle
Subject: Admin Fee Data Discovery
When: Friday, February 20, 2026 9:00 AM-10:00 AM (UTC-06:00) Central Time (US & Canada).
Where: Microsoft Teams Meeting

 

Bill,

 

Setting aside time as a second separate session in which I wanted to glean from your perspective the data that we have around Admin Fee. I have a few ‘loose’ requests to start the conversation that we can cover together, but overall, wanted to take a minute and understand the data that we have associated with this space.

 

Thanks!

Joe

Subject: Re: Pythian - GCP Premier Commitment Contract (2‑Year, $3.1M)

 

George - Is this budgeted and aligned with our forecasts?

From: Sanchez, Priscilla <Priscilla_Sanchez@PremierInc.com>
Sent: Thursday, February 26, 2026 10:05 AM
To: Climer, Crystal <Crystal_Climer@premierinc.com>
Cc: Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>; Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>; Schwering, Ben <Ben_Schwering@Premierinc.com>; Bava, Stephen <Stephen_Bava@PremierInc.com>; Williams, Evan <Evan_Williams@premierinc.com>
Subject: FW: Pythian - GCP Premier Commitment Contract (2‑Year, $3.1M) 

 

Hi Crystal,

 

We have finalized a 2‑year Google Cloud Premier Commitment Contract through Pythian, which has been fully negotiated and reviewed by legal and the leadership team.

 

Summary of the agreement

Term: 24 monthsCommitment: $3.1M minimum spendDiscount: 7% on servicesCounterparty: Pythian (direct agreement) 

Why this makes sense

We declined a direct renewal with Google due to significantly higher required commitment levels to maintain prior discounts.By contracting through Pythian, we were able to preserve a comparable discount while keeping the commitment lower and limiting duration to two years.The agreement is expected to generate approximately $217K in savings over the term versus a non‑discounted alternative.From a strategic standpoint, this aligns with our current cloud roadmap: while Databricks remains a long‑term consolidation objective, we have stable, cost‑effective BigQuery workloads that do not warrant near‑term migration. Leadership has confirmed that a 2‑year GCP commitment is appropriate given this posture. 

Status

The approach has been reviewed and approved by Bill, Matt, and Ben, with no outstanding concerns.Pythian has provided a clean, final version and is ready to route via DocuSign upon approval. 

I will route the attached amendment to you for signature via Docusign, please let us know if you have any questions. 

 

Thank you,

Priscilla

 

 

From: Schwering, Ben <Ben_Schwering@Premierinc.com> 
Sent: Thursday, February 26, 2026 8:46 AM
To: Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>; Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>; Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>
Cc: Sanchez, Priscilla <Priscilla_Sanchez@PremierInc.com>
Subject: Re: Pythian - GCP Premier Commitment Contract

 

I agree - a 2-year commitment makes sense. Let me know if I can help with the justification if you get any pushback.

 

Ben

 

From: Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>
Date: Thursday, February 26, 2026 at 8:28 AM
To: Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>, Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>, Schwering, Ben <Ben_Schwering@Premierinc.com>
Cc: Sanchez, Priscilla <Priscilla_Sanchez@PremierInc.com>
Subject: RE: Pythian - GCP Premier Commitment Contract

Nice work on the negotiations.

 

Your recommendation makes sense to me.   I do want to run this by Ben who’s leading the Databricks project.   We’ve said in the past that a full migration away from GCP is far down the list of priorities.   If that’s still the case, then a 2 yr commitment to GCP seems appropriate.    

 

@Schwering, Ben  Welcome your thoughts on this.

 

Thx

Bill

 

 

From: Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>
Sent: Wednesday, February 25, 2026 9:15 AM
To: Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>; Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>
Cc: Sanchez, Priscilla <Priscilla_Sanchez@PremierInc.com>
Subject: FW: Pythian - GCP Premier Commitment Contract

 

Matt, Bill,

 

Priscilla took the initiative to negotiate directly with Pythian to secure a 2-year recommitment for GCP at a $3.1M minimum spend with a 7% discount.

 

Why this is a good outcome:

As you’ll recall, we declined to renew directly with Google because sustaining our prior discounts required significantly higher commitment levels. Priscilla managed to secure nearly the same discount through a Pythian-driven commitment, keeping the overall spend on the lower end of our projections and limiting the duration to two years.

 

Expected financial impact:

If we assume no discounting as the alternative, this agreement should save roughly:

- $108.5K per year (7% of ~$1.55M/year implied by $3.1M over 24 months), or

- $217K over the 2-year term (7% of $3.1M).

 

Strategic context / why I recommend proceeding:

I recognize the organizational goal to consolidate to Databricks where feasible, and we’ll continue working toward that. However, we have significant BigQuery-based workloads that are stable, perform well, and would be more expensive and disruptive to migrate unnecessarily. For many of these, we can meet the “single plane of glass” objective by surfacing views into Databricks, avoiding a wholesale lift-and-shift of workloads that don’t need to move. Additionally, maintaining a meaningful presence with Google remains strategically useful for negotiation leverage, experimentation, and optionality as Google continues to evolve, especially given their position in the AI landscape. In that light, a 2-year commitment through Pythian (who also supports us operationally) feels like a prudent, low-regret decision.

 

Asks / next steps:

Please let me know if you’re comfortable with us proceeding on this approach. This is nearly ready to route for signature, and we’ll ultimately need Crystal’s sign-off.

 

Thanks,

Matt

bodyText:
George - Is this budgeted and aligned with our forecasts?

From: Sanchez, Priscilla <Priscilla_Sanchez@PremierInc.com>
Sent: Thursday, February 26, 2026 10:05 AM
To: Climer, Crystal <Crystal_Climer@premierinc.com>
Cc: Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>; Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>; Schwering, Ben <Ben_Schwering@Premierinc.com>; Bava, Stephen <Stephen_Bava@PremierInc.com>; Williams, Evan <Evan_Williams@premierinc.com>
Subject: FW: Pythian - GCP Premier Commitment Contract (2‑Year, $3.1M)  

Hi Crystal,

 

We have finalized a 2‑year Google Cloud Premier Commitment Contract through Pythian, which has been fully negotiated and reviewed by legal and the leadership team.

 

Summary of the agreement

Term: 24 monthsCommitment: $3.1M minimum spendDiscount: 7% on servicesCounterparty: Pythian (direct agreement) 

Why this makes sense

We declined a direct renewal with Google due to significantly higher required commitment levels to maintain prior discounts.By contracting through Pythian, we were able to preserve a comparable discount while keeping the commitment lower and limiting duration to two years.The agreement is expected to generate approximately $217K in savings over the term versus a non‑discounted alternative.From a strategic standpoint, this aligns with our current cloud roadmap: while Databricks remains a long‑term consolidation objective, we have stable, cost‑effective BigQuery workloads that do not warrant near‑term migration. Leadership has confirmed that a 2‑year GCP commitment is appropriate given this posture. 

Status

The approach has been reviewed and approved by Bill, Matt, and Ben, with no outstanding concerns.Pythian has provided a clean, final version and is ready to route via DocuSign upon approval. 

I will route the attached amendment to you for signature via Docusign, please let us know if you have any questions. 

 

Thank you,

Priscilla

 

 

From: Schwering, Ben <Ben_Schwering@Premierinc.com> 
Sent: Thursday, February 26, 2026 8:46 AM
To: Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>; Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>; Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>
Cc: Sanchez, Priscilla <Priscilla_Sanchez@PremierInc.com>
Subject: Re: Pythian - GCP Premier Commitment Contract

 

I agree - a 2-year commitment makes sense. Let me know if I can help with the justification if you get any pushback.

 

Ben

 

From: Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>
Date: Thursday, February 26, 2026 at 8:28 AM
To: Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>, Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>, Schwering, Ben <Ben_Schwering@Premierinc.com>
Cc: Sanchez, Priscilla <Priscilla_Sanchez@PremierInc.com>
Subject: RE: Pythian - GCP Premier Commitment Contract

Nice work on the negotiations.

 

Your recommendation makes sense to me.   I do want to run this by Ben who’s leading the Databricks project.   We’ve said in the past that a full migration away from GCP is far down the list of priorities.   If that’s still the case, then a 2 yr commitment to GCP seems appropriate.    

 

@Schwering, Ben  Welcome your thoughts on this.

 

Thx

Bill

 

 

From: Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>
Sent: Wednesday, February 25, 2026 9:15 AM
To: Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>; Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>
Cc: Sanchez, Priscilla <Priscilla_Sanchez@PremierInc.com>
Subject: FW: Pythian - GCP Premier Commitment Contract

 

Matt, Bill,

 

Priscilla took the initiative to negotiate directly with Pythian to secure a 2-year recommitment for GCP at a $3.1M minimum spend with a 7% discount.

 

Why this is a good outcome:

As you’ll recall, we declined to renew directly with Google because sustaining our prior discounts required significantly higher commitment levels. Priscilla managed to secure nearly the same discount through a Pythian-driven commitment, keeping the overall spend on the lower end of our projections and limiting the duration to two years.

 

Expected financial impact:

If we assume no discounting as the alternative, this agreement should save roughly:

- $108.5K per year (7% of ~$1.55M/year implied by $3.1M over 24 months), or

- $217K over the 2-year term (7% of $3.1M).

 

Strategic context / why I recommend proceeding:

I recognize the organizational goal to consolidate to Databricks where feasible, and we’ll continue working toward that. However, we have significant BigQuery-based workloads that are stable, perform well, and would be more expensive and disruptive to migrate unnecessarily. For many of these, we can meet the “single plane of glass” objective by surfacing views into Databricks, avoiding a wholesale lift-and-shift of workloads that don’t need to move. Additionally, maintaining a meaningful presence with Google remains strategically useful for negotiation leverage, experimentation, and optionality as Google continues to evolve, especially given their position in the AI landscape. In that light, a 2-year commitment through Pythian (who also supports us operationally) feels like a prudent, low-regret decision.

 

Asks / next steps:

Please let me know if you’re comfortable with us proceeding on this approach. This is nearly ready to route for signature, and we’ll ultimately need Crystal’s sign-off.

 

Thanks,

Matt

- $108.5K per year (7% of ~$1.55M/year implied by $3.1M over 24 months), or

- $217K over the 2-year term (7% of $3.1M).

 

Strategic context / why I recommend proceeding:

I recognize the organizational goal to consolidate to Databricks where feasible, and we’ll continue working toward that. However, we have significant BigQuery-based workloads that are stable, perform well, and would be more expensive and disruptive to migrate unnecessarily. For many of these, we can meet the “single plane of glass” objective by surfacing views into Databricks, avoiding a wholesale lift-and-shift of workloads that don’t need to move. Additionally, maintaining a meaningful presence with Google remains strategically useful for negotiation leverage, experimentation, and optionality as Google continues to evolve, especially given their position in the AI landscape. In that light, a 2-year commitment through Pythian (who also supports us operationally) feels like a prudent, low-regret decision.

 

Asks / next steps:

Please let me know if you’re comfortable with us proceeding on this approach. This is nearly ready to route for signature, and we’ll ultimately need Crystal’s sign-off.

 

Thanks,

Matt

Nice work on the negotiations.

 

Your recommendation makes sense to me.   I do want to run this by Ben who’s leading the Databricks project.   We’ve said in the past that a full migration away from GCP is far down the list of priorities.   If that’s still the case, then a 2 yr commitment to GCP seems appropriate.     

 

@Schwering, Ben  Welcome your thoughts on this.

 

Thx

Bill

Matt, Bill,

 

Priscilla took the initiative to negotiate directly with Pythian to secure a 2-year recommitment for GCP at a $3.1M minimum spend with a 7% discount.

 

Why this is a good outcome:

As you’ll recall, we declined to renew directly with Google because sustaining our prior discounts required significantly higher commitment levels. Priscilla managed to secure nearly the same discount through a Pythian-driven commitment, keeping the overall spend on the lower end of our projections and limiting the duration to two years.

 

Expected financial impact:

If we assume no discounting as the alternative, this agreement should save roughly:

- $108.5K per year (7% of ~$1.55M/year implied by $3.1M over 24 months), or

- $217K over the 2-year term (7% of $3.1M).

 

Strategic context / why I recommend proceeding:

I recognize the organizational goal to consolidate to Databricks where feasible, and we’ll continue working toward that. However, we have significant BigQuery-based workloads that are stable, perform well, and would be more expensive and disruptive to migrate unnecessarily. For many of these, we can meet the “single plane of glass” objective by surfacing views into Databricks, avoiding a wholesale lift-and-shift of workloads that don’t need to move. Additionally, maintaining a meaningful presence with Google remains strategically useful for negotiation leverage, experimentation, and optionality as Google continues to evolve, especially given their position in the AI landscape. In that light, a 2-year commitment through Pythian (who also supports us operationally) feels like a prudent, low-regret decision.

 

Asks / next steps:

Please let me know if you’re comfortable with us proceeding on this approach. This is nearly ready to route for signature, and we’ll ultimately need Crystal’s sign-off.

 

Thanks,

Matt

subject: February Leading at Premier: Peer Group Coaching and Discussion
bodyPreview:
Thank you to everyone who attended the Peer Group Coaching and Discussion Sessions in February!



Whether you attended in February or at any time recently, or listened to the recording,  please take this short survey (3 minutes) to provide feedba
bodyText:
Thank you to everyone who attended the Peer Group Coaching and Discussion Sessions in February!

 

Whether you attended in February or at any time recently, or listened to the recording,  please take this short survey (3 minutes) to provide feedback on the Leading at Premier Program. We want to know what you think of the three session formats we are using and to how we can improve the program as we all learn together.

 

What’s next?

 

Our topic in January and February was Collaboration as a Performance Driver. If you didn't attend the webinar in January or watch the 28-minute recording,  you still can at this link: Leading at Premier: Collaboration as a Performance Driver (Recording)
 

Print your Quick Cheat Sheet: Attached above is a one-page PDF of a Quick Cheat Sheet for Coaching for Effective Collaboration. You may want to print this out and have it handy or save it to your desktop. 
 

Check out the Resources Linked in the attached presentation:  In January, we presented a few resources that can support you in effective collaboration. Those include: ​RACI Matrix Template​, ​Real Talk Planning Tool​, and other learning resources for deeper learning on this topic.
 

Start thinking about questions and topics you want to bring to the Leading at Premier: Leadership Lab session you’ve been invited to in March. We will do a quick demo of a tool that will help you as you lead your team through change. 
 

Learn more about our Leading at Premier program and find recordings of past sessions on the HR Portal ​Leading at Premier page​. 

If you have any questions, please email HRInquiries@premierinc.com and we will make sure your question gets answered.

subject: FW: Pythian - GCP Premier Commitment Contract
bodyPreview:
Matt, Bill,

Priscilla took the initiative to negotiate directly with Pythian to secure a 2-year recommitment for GCP at a $3.1M minimum spend with a 7% discount.

Why this is a good outcome:
As you’ll recall, we declined to renew directly with Googl
bodyText:
Matt, Bill,

Priscilla took the initiative to negotiate directly with Pythian to secure a 2-year recommitment for GCP at a $3.1M minimum spend with a 7% discount.

Why this is a good outcome:
As you’ll recall, we declined to renew directly with Google because sustaining our prior discounts required significantly higher commitment levels. Priscilla managed to secure nearly the same discount through a Pythian-driven commitment, keeping the overall spend on the lower end of our projections and limiting the duration to two years.

Expected financial impact:
If we assume no discounting as the alternative, this agreement should save roughly:
- $108.5K per year (7% of ~$1.55M/year implied by $3.1M over 24 months), or
- $217K over the 2-year term (7% of $3.1M).

Strategic context / why I recommend proceeding:
I recognize the organizational goal to consolidate to Databricks where feasible, and we’ll continue working toward that. However, we have significant BigQuery-based workloads that are stable, perform well, and would be more expensive and disruptive to migrate unnecessarily. For many of these, we can meet the “single plane of glass” objective by surfacing views into Databricks, avoiding a wholesale lift-and-shift of workloads that don’t need to move. Additionally, maintaining a meaningful presence with Google remains strategically useful for negotiation leverage, experimentation, and optionality as Google continues to evolve, especially given their position in the AI landscape. In that light, a 2-year commitment through Pythian (who also supports us operationally) feels like a prudent, low-regret decision.

Asks / next steps:
Please let me know if you’re comfortable with us proceeding on this approach. This is nearly ready to route for signature, and we’ll ultimately need Crystal’s sign-off.

Thanks,
Matt

Hi Priscilla,

 

Thanks for your patience.  The incentives from Google are different now so we've been working to see how we can get the best deal possible for Premier.  

 

To reach a 10% level in BQ discounting, Premier will likely need to commit to a higher contract value closer to the range of $7.5m (over 3 years).  This is significantly more than the $6m proposal you asked for.  The reason for the higher TCV is that Google wants to see customer growth as part of a recommitment with higher incentives (even though it's 1 percentage more in BQ discounts that you're getting today).  

 

Is $7.5m something that Premier would be open to?  In the meantime I'm still pushing for a better proposal with Google.  

 

If you'd like to connect live over a call, my calendar is pretty flexible this Friday.  

 

- Benny

From: Hall, Brian <Brian_Hall@PremierInc.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 24, 2026 9:07 AM
To: Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>; Radcliff, Bruce <Bruce_Radcliff@premierinc.com>; Rounds, Caitlin <Caitlin_Rounds@premierinc.com>; Chrystal, Danielle <Danielle_Chrystal@premierinc.com>
Cc: Ralph, Joan <Joan_Ralph@PremierInc.com>; Schneider, Justin <Justin_Schneider@premierinc.com>; Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>; Lilly, Zach <Zach_Lilly@premierinc.com>; Daigle, Pam <Pamela_Daigle@PremierInc.com>; Hall, Brian <Brian_Hall@PremierInc.com>; Hendrix, Jennie <Jennie_Hendrix@premierinc.com>; Lehman, Molly <Molly_Lehman@PremierInc.com>; Garrett, Jordan <Jordan_Garrett@premierinc.com>; Bichler, Joe <Joe_Bichler@premierinc.com>
Subject: RE: Portfolio Expansion Update  

Morning Team –

 

Attached you will find the final high-level cut for Total Addressable Market across clinical, non-clinical, food and pharmacy along with and update on some of the progress made to date to create detailed drill views to begin to support Project Fusion.

 

Bill and I connected last night to discuss how this project overlaps with the Admin Fee Management project and agree we need to align the data/analytics resources across both projects as many of the inputs are the same and ultimately we need to create one data model to support both projects.  I also met with Joe B. to get an update on the Admin Fee Management project and will be starting to work with him to pull in the additional components he needs.  Next steps will be getting a few of us integrated into the two core work teams and developing a plan for rolling out the details we have pulled together to gain more insights at the service line levels.

 

Thanks,

Brian

 

Brian Hall, MBA, CMRP, MCP, MCAD
Vice President – Strategic Sourcing Operations & Analytics - Supply Chain Services

Office: 704.816.5528 | eFax: 704.733.2062
brian_hall@premierinc.com  |  Premier, Inc. (NASDAQ: PINC)

Sr. Administrative Coordinator:  Melissa Smith  Melissa_Smith@premierinc.com 

 

From: Hall, Brian 
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 5:37 PM
To: Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>; Radcliff, Bruce <Bruce_Radcliff@premierinc.com>
Cc: Ralph, Joan <Joan_Ralph@PremierInc.com>; Lough, Robert <Bob_Lough@PremierInc.com>; Merendino, John <John_Merendino@PremierInc.com>; Schneider, Justin <Justin_Schneider@premierinc.com>; Scavo, Shelley <Shelley_Scavo@PremierInc.com>; Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>; Lilly, Zach <Zach_Lilly@premierinc.com>; Daigle, Pam <Pamela_Daigle@PremierInc.com>; Hall, Brian <Brian_Hall@PremierInc.com>; Hendrix, Jennie <Jennie_Hendrix@premierinc.com>; Lehman, Molly <Molly_Lehman@PremierInc.com>; Garrett, Jordan <Jordan_Garrett@premierinc.com>
Subject: RE: Portfolio Expansion Update

 

Bill and Bruce –

Wanted to provide you both with a DRAFT summary of the Total Addressable Market and Portfolio Expansion potential as we close out the week.

 

The team has been working through the various datasets to identify total addressable market and the four primary buckets of On Contract, Off Contract, Non Contract and Unknown.  Below is an update to as to what we have identified as of this afternoon, though this is draft as we do expect a couple of updates on Monday relative to Food as John M. is working to identify untapped market for acute and senior living (yellow highlights subject to change).  Additionally, the pharmacy team may want to make small refinements to their model after they talk further on Monday.  More work needs to continue into the next couple of weeks on the core data to allow for additional drill downs, but we feel confident in the numbers below as a high-level rollup to start.

 

Additional background on methodology used to date is included in the attached slides.  I would be remiss if I didn’t give a shout out to everyone copied  for the cross collaborative efforts and quickly jumping in to help us review each business unit.

 

Please let us know if you have additional questions.  We will send another update next week with more details as everything is refined and finalized.

 

Thanks,
Brian

 

 

Brian Hall, MBA, CMRP, MCP, MCAD
Vice President – Strategic Sourcing Operations & Analytics - Supply Chain Services

Office: 704.816.5528 | eFax: 704.733.2062
brian_hall@premierinc.com  |  Premier, Inc. (NASDAQ: PINC)

Subject: Re: Portfolio Expansion Update

 

I like the grid below.    I think we need to show progress to Bruce tomorrow.   He asks daily for a status!    For Non-Clinical, can you break it into buckets based on what we know today.   I realize that means there will be a huge amount in the Unknown bucket, but we should be able to guesstimate TAM.   And then over the next week, we can further classify into the other buckets.

 

Thanks everyone!

Bill

Our current dashboard breaks off contract into three distinct buckets as there’s different action items needed for each.

Off contract, non-contract category: this represents spend that is not currently on contract with Premier and Premier does not have any GPO contracts in that category space today. Essentially, this is spend that we have no way to address at this time. An example would be surgical robotics as a category as we do not have any vendors on contract in that space that compete with intuitive and the da Vinci.  This maps to the red noncontract on your slideOff contract, non-contracted supplier: this represents spend that is off contract from Premier, where we do not have the supplier on contract that the member is purchasing from today, however, we do have competitive suppliers on contract in that specific category that a member could potentially convert to.  This maps to the yellow off contract on your slide.Off contract, SKU not on contract: This represents spend that is Potentially off contract from Premier, even though we have the supplier on contract and offer a category for the item being purchased. There are times when suppliers refuse to put their entire portfolio products on our GPO agreement and that is where these items are bucketed. Relative to spend impact and admin fees, some of the sales we do receive admin fees on per the terms of the agreement and others we do not. An example of this could be some of the filters or other items on the patient warming devices that are really more of a repair/maintenance product and not something sold in the main stream.  This could potentially map to the health contract or on contract bucket on the slide, depending upon if you're looking at the terms where they pay on all sales, regardless of SKUs on contract or if the language dictates, they only pay on sales excuses on the actual agreementThe last bucket that I would say is missing is the unknown spend where we receive it in our member submission files however we are unable to make heads or tails of it from a category or supplier perspectiveThanks,

It does not try to estimate the substantial additional volume in the non-OLM CoC space, or the additional volume from health systems that use only the Rx / Food / Conductiv programs.

 

That said, here's a picture that helps visualize the relative size of the four service lines (within this segment), and clarifies where we need to rely on the wholesaler / food distributor tracings (left side) vs. where we can leverage a subset of the Supply Analytics (right side) data:

From: Lilly, Zach <Zach_Lilly@PremierInc.com>
Date: Thursday, February 12, 2026 at 2:25 PM
To: Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>
Cc: Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>, Hall, Brian <Brian_Hall@PremierInc.com>, Garrett, Jordan <Jordan_Garrett@PremierInc.com>, Hendrix, Jennie <Jennie_Hendrix@PremierInc.com>, Kapu, UdayKumar <UdayKumar_Kapu@PremierInc.com>
Subject: Portfolio Expansion Update

Hey Bill,

 

Good afternoon! We wanted to share a quick update on the analytical groundwork we've been building for the Portfolio Expansion initiative.

 

What we did

We conducted a detailed analysis of invoice data (ERP + Remitra) to establish a reliable benchmark for how a typical health system’s non-labor purchasing distributes across service lines (clinical, non-clinical, pharmacy, and food). While the invoice data is not sufficiently granular for product- or category-level analysis (i.e., on- vs. off-contract), it is detailed enough to establish a reliable service line mix.

 

What this enables

1. Expected volume targets by service line across the full GPO membership

By applying the service line mix to Premier's ~1,775 core GPO hospitals (224K staffed beds), we estimate $122-132B in total non-labor purchasing:$52-57B in clinical$50-54B in non-clinical$18-19B in pharmacy$2B in foodThese benchmarks also establish a reference point for when we pivot to more detailed data sources for future on-/off-/non-contract analysis, helping us identify service line gaps 

2. Extension of Supply Analytics coverage to non-clinical

We identified 100 health systems with comprehensive reporting across both clinical and non-clinical spend (the member-submitted and extracted PO/invoice blend)This allows us to extend existing clinical contract coverage analysis to non-clinical categories immediately (i.e., Brian Hall's team's visualizations), without additional data sourcingBecause this subset represents ~80% of observed GPO clinical volume, we can extrapolate findings to the full membership when we need to estimate, for example, how much additional GPO volume could be brought under contract by expanding coverage across these two service lines (and, what that means in terms of admin fees & growth opportunities) 

Note: This analysis focuses on health system and hospital purchasing (the spend flowing through ERPs). The non-acute / alternate site market (retail pharmacy, specialty, etc.) sits outside this framework and would require a separate approach, which we can address in a future phase.

 

Next steps

Upon receipt of the non-clinical "catch-all" category breakdowns (i.e., splitting IT into more discreet categories), we will begin decomposing subcategories to size on-/off-/non-contract opportunity within the non-clinical space. These breakdowns are expected to be delivered by tomorrow.

 

Please let us know if there are any questions!

 

Best,

Zach Lilly

Senior Technical Business Analyst

Advanced Business Intelligence (ABI) Team

bodyText:
Good afternoon,

 

I hope this email finds you all well!

 

I wanted to send a note to this team acknowledging Andrea Lewis’ upcoming leave March 2- April 6 and discuss back-up support. As most of you are aware, Jolene Smith, who supported the SSE Team and Pam Daigle, departed the SCS Admin team in December 2025. Her support desk has been transitioned over to myself, Peg Schwartz and Kena Rodman in the interim until approval to backfill her role is granted. Absorbing Jolene’s support desk has added a high amount of additional workload to our small team and with Andrea taking time away,  we will be down another Coordinator for a period of time. While my team and I are always here to support you in the best possible way, I’m hoping you all could please help us by directly handling minor tasks or delegating them to a member of your team while Andrea is out of office and coming to the admin team for larger/complex meeting needs or urgent needs only. We, of course, are here for other requests/questions, please just be aware that response/action time may be delayed due to workload. 

 

Please know I’m here to partner with each of you to ensure your support needs are met. I sincerely appreciate your understanding and if anyone needs to discuss more in depth or has questions, please feel free to reach out to me.

 

Thank you!

 

Melissa R. Smith

bodyText:
Hi Bill and team,

I think a portion of this was ironed out in the sync-up call I hosted this morning. To level set for the group: I had a call with the point-people already working through the data on the Growth project to ensure that the tentacles created from this first push to gather and process data is pulled back toward the center.

That said, I am expanding on your points, Bill, and addressing as a follow-up from our call.

Data Model for Growth & Admin Fee:
I do think we should review the specific outcome needed for each data ask within the two projects and determine where there are possible efficiencies.
NEXT STEPS: Danielle, Justin, Joe, and I will meet to understand the specific deliverables from the data tasks on both projects. We will outline next steps and drive the respective projects forward to capitalize on data efficiencies, if there are any, from the same work plan.
I do also think that the datapoint needed for the Admin Fee team may already be pulled into the data compiled for the Growth team - we will look into this specifically.
Core Team Members:
If they are working on the project as a core team member, I need them included in the stakeholder list so that they are part of the right communications.
This also gives access to the Monday.com board, which has tasks and timelines outlined for this work being done.
CONFIRMING:
Clinical & Non-Clinical Data Team: Brian Hall, Jennie Hendrix
Pharmacy: Ed Zagorsky, Michael Scanlon, Shelley Scavo
Food: John Merendino, TBD (if needed)
Structure & Presentation:
The data needs to be comprehensive across each of the BUs and the whole of Supply Chain, but the views needed will have inputs from the core team familiar with the scope of the strategic objectives and signed off by Justin.
I do not think that Brian and/or others should be communicating out, or setting standardized structures, templates, etc. as this is only one small part of the project. The strategic vision and how we need the data to speak to it is what Justin as the 'Growth' project leader is going to be able to advise on, so we align on hitting goals across all levels. We need to review and align with him and his delegate(s).
NEXT STEPS: 
Caitlin to sync with Justin on current-state for the Data work
Caitlin and Danielle to meet with Brian (other new Core Team members)
Caitlin to host the cadence for the Dashboarding & Metrics Core Team, including Justin
Dash & Metrics Workstream's End-Goal Objectives:
Raw data (unfiltered, unsanitized) pulled together to show specific views that can be used in analysis
Dashboard(s) created off data to be pulled routinely and show trends against growth targets and KPIs
Individual business unit plans on where to focus growth efforts based on the data and the stories it tells

Reach out and let Justin or myself know if there are questions or concerns. As always, @Schneider, Justin is the project leader and any guidance from him are our 'north star'. Please adjust/feedback as you see fit, Justin!

Thanks,

Caitlin

Bill - the note highlighted below is key - the estimation of total “GPO member” volume is specific to just health systems (hospitals & a little OLM non-acute) that are part of the core Premier GPO (i.e. clinical). It does not try to estimate the substantial additional volume in the non-OLM CoC space, or the additional volume from health systems that use only the Rx / Food / Conductiv programs.

 

That said, here's a picture that helps visualize the relative size of the four service lines (within this segment), and clarifies where we need to rely on the wholesaler / food distributor tracings (left side) vs. where we can leverage a subset of the Supply Analytics (right side) data:

 

Based on the estimated ~$127B total annual spend across all four SLs for just core-GPO health system members, the theoretical ceiling for annual NAF is ~$1.52 billion (assuming a blended 3% gross fee and 60% shareback). This establishes the total addressable market, where every 1% of volume we win or convert is worth roughly $15 million to the bottom line.

 

Matt

 

From: Lilly, Zach <Zach_Lilly@PremierInc.com>
Date: Thursday, February 12, 2026 at 2:25 PM
To: Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>
Cc: Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>, Hall, Brian <Brian_Hall@PremierInc.com>, Garrett, Jordan <Jordan_Garrett@PremierInc.com>, Hendrix, Jennie <Jennie_Hendrix@PremierInc.com>, Kapu, UdayKumar <UdayKumar_Kapu@PremierInc.com>
Subject: Portfolio Expansion Update

Hey Bill,

 

Good afternoon! We wanted to share a quick update on the analytical groundwork we've been building for the Portfolio Expansion initiative.

 

What we did

We conducted a detailed analysis of invoice data (ERP + Remitra) to establish a reliable benchmark for how a typical health system’s non-labor purchasing distributes across service lines (clinical, non-clinical, pharmacy, and food). While the invoice data is not sufficiently granular for product- or category-level analysis (i.e., on- vs. off-contract), it is detailed enough to establish a reliable service line mix.

 

What this enables

1. Expected volume targets by service line across the full GPO membership

By applying the service line mix to Premier's ~1,775 core GPO hospitals (224K staffed beds), we estimate $122-132B in total non-labor purchasing:$52-57B in clinical$50-54B in non-clinical$18-19B in pharmacy$2B in foodThese benchmarks also establish a reference point for when we pivot to more detailed data sources for future on-/off-/non-contract analysis, helping us identify service line gaps 

2. Extension of Supply Analytics coverage to non-clinical

We identified 100 health systems with comprehensive reporting across both clinical and non-clinical spend (the member-submitted and extracted PO/invoice blend)This allows us to extend existing clinical contract coverage analysis to non-clinical categories immediately (i.e., Brian Hall's team's visualizations), without additional data sourcingBecause this subset represents ~80% of observed GPO clinical volume, we can extrapolate findings to the full membership when we need to estimate, for example, how much additional GPO volume could be brought under contract by expanding coverage across these two service lines (and, what that means in terms of admin fees & growth opportunities) 

Note: This analysis focuses on health system and hospital purchasing (the spend flowing through ERPs). The non-acute / alternate site market (retail pharmacy, specialty, etc.) sits outside this framework and would require a separate approach, which we can address in a future phase.

 

Next steps

Upon receipt of the non-clinical "catch-all" category breakdowns (i.e., splitting IT into more discreet categories), we will begin decomposing subcategories to size on-/off-/non-contract opportunity within the non-clinical space. These breakdowns are expected to be delivered by tomorrow.

 

Please let us know if there are any questions!

 

Best,

Zach Lilly

Senior Technical Business Analyst

Advanced Business Intelligence (ABI) Team

subject: Data and Analytics to support strategic initiatives
bodyPreview:
Brian and I chatted for a while last night about the best way to support Justin and Joe.   A couple notes.


  *   We should think of this as one data model that supports both initiatives.  Revenue is tied to rate and volume, so we should maintain one
bodyText:
Brian and I chatted for a while last night about the best way to support Justin and Joe.   A couple notes.

 

We should think of this as one data model that supports both initiatives.  Revenue is tied to rate and volume, so we should maintain one solution with consistent nomenclature.   So, rather than address Joe’s recent request in isolation I recommend that the Admin Fee rate data he needs be included in the model that Brian is maintaining.  (It may already be there).There are deep domain experts who need to do the category level analysis that’s requiredBrian & Team will go deep on Clinical and non-Clinical, and evolve the analytics as requiredEd Zagorsky & Team do the same for PharmacyWe need to figure out who owns Food!We need a standard and simple way to roll up the analysis for Bruce and Karr.    Anything we show needs to look like one cohesive initiative and not fragmented work.Brian will take lead on defining the standard for other teams to use.   If everyone adheres to that standard and rolls up their analysis consistently, we should be able to give Bruce what he needs.The ABI team is available to help if teams need more data or require any technical support.Once we build out this model, we can figure out how (if?) to harden and scale into true product. 

If we’re in agreement, we need to ensure that Brian (or his designee) is an integral part of both teams.

 

 

 

Bill Marquardt

Group Vice President, Digital Supply Chain

Purpose:  Today’s call is a follow up to the meeting with Philips’ leadership at the JPMorgan conference.  Per Kristine Mullen, she has aligned with their Monitoring business to see how they can leverage our data and insights alongside their own to drive opportunities forward.  There is interest and they believe we can connect more deeply here.

·         Implication: If the capital equipment (and accessories, etc.) of interest is generally contracted with Premier, then the Market Insights has it covered. Within the acute setting, the MI data includes more volume, and provides much richer insight, than manufacturer-reported sales tracings.

 

Needs:  Philips has expressed a need for more visibility to their products/usage plus more insight into competitor landscape that could lead to improved Philips market position and action plans.

Subject: Introducing John Little

 

Please join me in welcoming John Little to Premier.  John will be supporting the AllSpire Members as Vice President Account Executive.  Reporting to John will be Amanda Whiffing, Jennifer Taylor, and Angela Burkholder.  John’s experience will allow us to improve our overall partnership with AllSpire and its Members.  

 

John is a seasoned healthcare executive with more than 30 years of leadership experience in supply chain, procurement, contracting, and GPO engagement. Before joining Premier, he served in a senior leadership role at Jubilant Radiopharma, a national provider of radiopharmaceuticals and nuclear medicine services. Throughout his career, he has consistently driven value creation, strengthened supply and contract performance, and built effective partnerships with health systems, GPOs, and industry stakeholders.

 

He previously held executive roles including Executive Vice President at CoalesCo, Inc., where he led two regional GPOs—Cooperative Services of Florida, Inc. and SCM Alliance, Inc.—overseeing operations, contracting strategy, and portfolio development across key healthcare categories. Prior to CoalesCo, he served as Senior Director of Supply Chain at Vizient. His expertise includes developing high‑performing teams, aligning financial and clinical priorities, and advancing innovative supply chain strategies.

 

John lives in Buffalo, NY; his wife is an executive chef and has two children in college. He is an avid outdoors enthusiast who enjoys camping, hiking, and golf. He is also a committed fan of the Ole Miss Rebels and the Buffalo Bills. Outside of work, he mentors emerging supply chain professionals and remains active in healthcare leadership initiatives.

 

Welcome John!

 

Kevin

 

Kevin DeBruyne

East Zone General Manager

Premier, Inc.

Cc: Rollins, Rachael <Rachael_Rollins@PremierInc.com>; Chrystal, Danielle <Danielle_Chrystal@premierinc.com>
Subject: RE: Admin Fee Data Discovery 

 

Bill, 

 

Thanks for the collaboration today. 

 

As a follow-up for the data collection aspect of the admin fee rate, below are some thoughts generated from this exercise about data gathering phase. Would value you, your teams, etc insights on if there are components to be a value add if there are gaps as well. 

 

 

 

Current State Data Collection Exercise 

Current Goal/Purpose: Baseline measurement of each Service Line, Program Offering, Supplier, Teammate, Leader rolled up into a drillable report based on the below requirements.  

Future Goal : We will need to be ability to have this report be an interactive dashboard that we can pull up and filter/drill where needed. The ability to have it be refreshed on a predetermined cycle (i.e. nightly) to update.  

 

Requirements / The Ask. Thinking about having 4 different views of a similar baseline data report that have similar ingredients. Just rolling it up into different views. You have offered strong advice on how to look at this, so truly open to alternative solutions here but intent is to get a baseline to get the leaders to agree on a baseline / day 0.  

Service Line Report at the header level by each service line.  Ex. Pharmacy, Food, Clinical, Non-Clinical, and Technology.                                                              i.      Additionally, request to be drillable into sub service line. Ex. Clinical drills to Surgical. Non-Clinical can be drilled into Construction, etc.

                                                            ii.      Additionally, want to creatively drill then here by supplier as well. We probably need to mock it up together to see what works.  

                                                          iii.      Data Points included in the report: sliced by sales volume, by gross admin fee (GAF), by weighted average % (WAAF)), …. Then also by columns of other revenue. To your point, can we create some columns of alternative review like MSA’s, Market Insights, etc….. Also want to include the supplier names  

                                                           iv.      Historical: Would like to see if we can slice it by month, quarter, fiscal year … and include some sort of trending component here dating back… if possible… 5 years? 

 

Programmatic View: Would see if we can roll up a report by programmatic offering like SURPASS, AscenDrive, and National / Other program offerings by division.                                                               i.      Same as above. Service Lines if we can and sub service line. Data points are similar and the historical. 

 

Supplier Report Report: Looking for a separate report here that you can toggle between to see by supplier overall rather than division of premier.                                                               i.      Data Points: To your point, want to think about sales volume, by gross admin fee (GAF), by weighted average % (WAAF)) but also alternative revenue like MSA’s, market insights, etc. 

                                                            ii.      Historical: Would like to see if we can slice it by month, quarter, fiscal year … and include some sort of trending component here dating back… if possible… 5 years?

 

Team Member / Leader Report : Looking for a report here that we can identify by team member what the admin fee they have generated.                                                               i.      Data Points: Interested to pull down how many contracts they wrote, what was the admin fee average rate, the volume projected or actual off that contract /admin fee. Probably some of the same indications aboe around Sales volume, GAF, WAAF, MSA’s, Market Insights, etc. 

                                                            ii.      Data: would also like to see if we can try to line them up to the service line, sub service line 

                                                          iii.      Data: wonder if we can toss in if they contract for a program like SURPASS 

                                                           iv.      Historical: Would like to see if we can slice it by the same. Month, quarter, fiscal year, etc. dating back a few years if possible. 

 

 

 

Requirements: Team member contract KPI's. How many contracts they wrote, what admin fee was associated, and the volume (projected & actual) off that contract. Dating back 5 years ago with rolling data going forward ability to see trending and filters by month, quarter, Fiscal year. Ability to see by service line they belong to and sub service line as well as if they belong to a program like SURPASS.

bodyText:
Hi everyone,

Please see notes below from Katelin and please forward this email to anyone I may have left off. 

 

 

Thank you for joining us yesterday for our Breakthroughs 26 supplier info session. If you were unable to join us, the link to the recording is HERE and we encourage you to take a listen so you're up to speed!

 

Attached is our PPT presentation from yesterday as well as our prospectus and save the date image for your own use. 

 

Below is our most recent update we shared with the Commercial team for some additional information regarding sessions, etc. We are working on a Breakthroughs promo toolkit for you all to use and will be back in touch soon! 

 

BREAKTHROUGHS 26 Supplier Registration is LIVE!

Early bird supplier registration is open! Please encourage your suppliers to register before early bird pricing and perks end March 31!

Member registration will go live in May. Stay tuned for more information! 

 

https://breakthroughs.premierinc.com/

 

BREAKTHROUGHS 26 MEMBER EDUCATION

The Education Team is gearing up for Breakthroughs 2026, October 6–8 in National Harbor, MD!
Submission portal is open: January 15 – March 31.
Sessions will be formatted in either a panel discussion, focused learning session, or a solution spotlight.

Panel Discussions: For the Executive, Clinical Excellence, Supply Chain Innovation and Workforce Reinvention tracks, selected proposals will be grouped into dynamic 60-minute, moderator-led panel discussions. Each panel will feature three to four speakers from different organizations sharing strategies, successes and lessons learned.Focused Learning Sessions: For Pharmacy, Food Service, Continuum of Care and Premier Applied Sciences, session formats will be determined by the respective teams and may include one to two speakers, depending on the topic and content.Solution Spotlights: These 30-minute sessions highlight how a solution addressed a key challenge, improved outcomes or created efficiencies others can replicate.The latest email promo was sent to members on Thursday, Feb. 12.
Details can be found here. 
Questions: Kelly Hicks , Nicole Verrone

 

General Breakthroughs questions can go to breakthroughs_conference@premierinc.com.

 

 

We appreciate your support!

 

Thank you, 

 

Katelin Glinka

Sr. Director, Brand Experience & Events

Marketing | Premier Inc.

M: 704.778.2444

premierinc.com

+Stay Connected: X | LinkedIn | Instagram | Facebook

+2026 Breakthroughs Conference and Exhibition | October 6-8, 2026

bodyText:
Hi Carlos,

We aren’t able to support a short-term bridge for continuity. As presented, it creates operational lift on our side without economics our leadership would approve.

We’re open to moving forward if there’s a commercially viable plan. To evaluate quickly, we’d need a proposal with defined scope, structure, and economics.

If you’re able to put that together (can be a draft version) we’d welcome the opportunity to review and see if there’s a path forward.

Kind regards,

Kari

Hi Kari, 

 

Thank you for the quick reply. 

 

The change from the historical arrangement creates a real discontinuity for customers, and we need a workable, orderly path that avoids unnecessary disruption. Given that an extension is not possible we would welcome any ideas for a smooth transition that better align with your long-term model. Even if we do not call it a “bridge,” a short, controlled mechanism that gives customers time to transition while Premier implements a longer-term model.

 

We would appreciate your thoughts and happy to schedule some time to brainstorm ideas.  

 

Best regards, 

Carlos 

 

From: Kalgren, Kari <Kari_Kalgren@PremierInc.com> 
Sent: 18 February 2026 17:14
To: Carlos Granja <carlos.granja@clarivate.com>; Andrew Lee <andrew.lee@clarivate.com>
Cc: Maloney, Myla <Myla_Maloney@premierinc.com>; Schmidt, Lindsay <Lindsay_Schmidt@PremierInc.com>; Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>; Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>; Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>; Juliano, Denise <Denise_Juliano@premierinc.com>; House, John <John_House@PremierInc.com>
Subject: Re: Next Steps: Premier Data Contract 

 

Hi Carlos,

Thank you for the thoughtful discussion and for working through options with us. After internal review, we aren’t able to offer a transition or bridge structure.

Our position remains aligned to the proposal we previously shared, which reflects the long-term model we are moving toward. If that framework works for Clarivate, we’d be glad to continue the conversation and move forward together.

We understand this represents a change from the historical arrangement and appreciate the partnership to date. Regardless of the outcome here, we remain open to exploring future ways to collaborate where our strategies align.

Best regards,
Kari

 

 

From: Carlos Granja <carlos.granja@clarivate.com>
Sent: Wednesday, February 18, 2026 10:03 AM
To: Kalgren, Kari <Kari_Kalgren@PremierInc.com>; Andrew Lee <Andrew.Lee@Clarivate.com>
Cc: Maloney, Myla <Myla_Maloney@premierinc.com>; Schmidt, Lindsay <Lindsay_Schmidt@PremierInc.com>; Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>; Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>; Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>; Juliano, Denise <Denise_Juliano@premierinc.com>; House, John <John_House@PremierInc.com>
Subject: RE: Next Steps: Premier Data Contract 

 

Hi Kari, We have been working on our transition plan and communicating this change to our customers. I am sure you appreciate that a major disruption in a key data asset that we've been using for nearly a decade (Market Insights/Supply Chain)

Hi Kari,

 

We have been working on our transition plan and communicating this change to our customers. I am sure you appreciate that a major disruption in a key data asset that we've been using for nearly a decade (Market Insights/Supply Chain) is a significant challenge and therefore we are looking for ways to facilitate a smoother transition for our clients. With that in mind, would Premier consider a 9-month bridge designed specifically to support an orderly transition? If extending the current structure is not feasible, we would be open to exploring any alternative mechanisms preferred by Premier that could achieve the same objective.

 

Lastly, we are still interested in the potential of PHD/clinical data and remain hopeful that we can build a strong partnership in the future. Andrew debriefed me on some of the ideas that came out of your meeting in Charlotte and we are keen to maintain our connection and work out something in the future that can support the industry in greater ways than we are doing individually.

 

Best regards, 

Carlos

 

From: Carlos Granja 
Sent: 05 February 2026 15:02
To: 'Kalgren, Kari' <Kari_Kalgren@PremierInc.com>; Andrew Lee <Andrew.Lee@Clarivate.com>
Cc: Maloney, Myla <Myla_Maloney@premierinc.com>; Schmidt, Lindsay <Lindsay_Schmidt@PremierInc.com>; Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>; Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>; Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>; Juliano, Denise <Denise_Juliano@premierinc.com>; House, John <John_House@PremierInc.com>
Subject: RE: Next Steps: Premier Data Contract 

 

Hi Kari, 

 

Thank you for checking internally and responding so promptly. We truly appreciate all the support you have provided, and we look forward to future opportunities for collaboration. 

 

Noted on the last data delivery date.

 

Best regards,

Carlos 

 

From: Kalgren, Kari <Kari_Kalgren@PremierInc.com> 
Sent: 04 February 2026 21:42
To: Andrew Lee <andrew.lee@clarivate.com>; Carlos Granja <carlos.granja@clarivate.com>
Cc: Maloney, Myla <Myla_Maloney@premierinc.com>; Schmidt, Lindsay <Lindsay_Schmidt@PremierInc.com>; Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>; Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>; Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>; Juliano, Denise <Denise_Juliano@premierinc.com>; House, John <John_House@PremierInc.com>
Subject: Next Steps: Premier Data Contract 

 

Hi Andrew & Carlos,

 

Thank you for the continued dialogue over the past several months. We appreciate the time and thought both teams invested in exploring potential paths forward.

 

While we explored a number of options and concessions in good faith, we were ultimately unable to identify a viable structure that aligned with the value of the broader enterprise data bundle or our long-term commercialization strategy. As a result, our leadership has confirmed that we’re not able to move forward with an extension or restructuring of the proposal we offered to you.

 

The existing contract will expire as scheduled on March 30, 2026. The final Market Insights data delivery will be the last week of March. No additional data will be provided beyond that contractual term. Market Insights as a product is being sunset as a standalone offering in this channel, which further limits our ability to revisit this engagement in its current form.

 

I have valued the collaboration with you both since joining the discussions. While this particular product and structure are concluding, we remain open to future conversations should there be alignment with Clarivate’s evolving data strategy and business needs. 

 

Best regards,

Kari

Cc: Maloney, Myla <Myla_Maloney@premierinc.com>; Schmidt, Lindsay <Lindsay_Schmidt@PremierInc.com>; Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>; Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>; Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>; Juliano, Denise <Denise_Juliano@premierinc.com>; House, John <John_House@PremierInc.com>
Subject: Next Steps: Premier Data Contract 

 

Hi Andrew & Carlos,

 

Thank you for the continued dialogue over the past several months. We appreciate the time and thought both teams invested in exploring potential paths forward.

 

While we explored a number of options and concessions in good faith, we were ultimately unable to identify a viable structure that aligned with the value of the broader enterprise data bundle or our long-term commercialization strategy. As a result, our leadership has confirmed that we’re not able to move forward with an extension or restructuring of the proposal we offered to you.

 

The existing contract will expire as scheduled on March 30, 2026. The final Market Insights data delivery will be the last week of March. No additional data will be provided beyond that contractual term. Market Insights as a product is being sunset as a standalone offering in this channel, which further limits our ability to revisit this engagement in its current form.

 

I have valued the collaboration with you both since joining the discussions. While this particular product and structure are concluding, we remain open to future conversations should there be alignment with Clarivate’s evolving data strategy and business needs. 

 

Best regards,

Kari

Hi Kari, 

 

Thank you for the quick reply. 

 

The change from the historical arrangement creates a real discontinuity for customers, and we need a workable, orderly path that avoids unnecessary disruption. Given that an extension is not possible we would welcome any ideas for a smooth transition that better align with your long-term model. Even if we do not call it a “bridge,” a short, controlled mechanism that gives customers time to transition while Premier implements a longer-term model.

 

We would appreciate your thoughts and happy to schedule some time to brainstorm ideas.  

 

Best regards, 

Carlos 

 

From: Kalgren, Kari <Kari_Kalgren@PremierInc.com> 
Sent: 18 February 2026 17:14
To: Carlos Granja <carlos.granja@clarivate.com>; Andrew Lee <andrew.lee@clarivate.com>
Cc: Maloney, Myla <Myla_Maloney@premierinc.com>; Schmidt, Lindsay <Lindsay_Schmidt@PremierInc.com>; Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>; Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>; Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>; Juliano, Denise <Denise_Juliano@premierinc.com>; House, John <John_House@PremierInc.com>
Subject: Re: Next Steps: Premier Data Contract 

 

Hi Carlos,

Thank you for the thoughtful discussion and for working through options with us. After internal review, we aren’t able to offer a transition or bridge structure.

Our position remains aligned to the proposal we previously shared, which reflects the long-term model we are moving toward. If that framework works for Clarivate, we’d be glad to continue the conversation and move forward together.

We understand this represents a change from the historical arrangement and appreciate the partnership to date. Regardless of the outcome here, we remain open to exploring future ways to collaborate where our strategies align.

Best regards,
Kari

Hi Kari,

 

We have been working on our transition plan and communicating this change to our customers. I am sure you appreciate that a major disruption in a key data asset that we've been using for nearly a decade (Market Insights/Supply Chain) is a significant challenge and therefore we are looking for ways to facilitate a smoother transition for our clients. With that in mind, would Premier consider a 9-month bridge designed specifically to support an orderly transition? If extending the current structure is not feasible, we would be open to exploring any alternative mechanisms preferred by Premier that could achieve the same objective.

 

Lastly, we are still interested in the potential of PHD/clinical data and remain hopeful that we can build a strong partnership in the future. Andrew debriefed me on some of the ideas that came out of your meeting in Charlotte and we are keen to maintain our connection and work out something in the future that can support the industry in greater ways than we are doing individually.

 

Best regards, 

Carlos

Hi Kari,

 

We have been working on our transition plan and communicating this change to our customers. I am sure you appreciate that a major disruption in a key data asset that we've been using for nearly a decade (Market Insights/Supply Chain) is a significant challenge and therefore we are looking for ways to facilitate a smoother transition for our clients. With that in mind, would Premier consider a 9-month bridge designed specifically to support an orderly transition? If extending the current structure is not feasible, we would be open to exploring any alternative mechanisms preferred by Premier that could achieve the same objective.

 

Lastly, we are still interested in the potential of PHD/clinical data and remain hopeful that we can build a strong partnership in the future. Andrew debriefed me on some of the ideas that came out of your meeting in Charlotte and we are keen to maintain our connection and work out something in the future that can support the industry in greater ways than we are doing individually.

 

Best regards, 

Carlos

Hi Kari,

 

We have been working on our transition plan and communicating this change to our customers. I am sure you appreciate that a major disruption in a key data asset that we've been using for nearly a decade (Market Insights/Supply Chain) is a significant challenge and therefore we are looking for ways to facilitate a smoother transition for our clients. With that in mind, would Premier consider a 9-month bridge designed specifically to support an orderly transition? If extending the current structure is not feasible, we would be open to exploring any alternative mechanisms preferred by Premier that could achieve the same objective.

 

Lastly, we are still interested in the potential of PHD/clinical data and remain hopeful that we can build a strong partnership in the future. Andrew debriefed me on some of the ideas that came out of your meeting in Charlotte and we are keen to maintain our connection and work out something in the future that can support the industry in greater ways than we are doing individually.

 

Best regards, 

Carlos

From: Kalgren, Kari <Kari_Kalgren@PremierInc.com> 
Sent: 04 February 2026 21:42
To: Andrew Lee <andrew.lee@clarivate.com>; Carlos Granja <carlos.granja@clarivate.com>
Cc: Maloney, Myla <Myla_Maloney@premierinc.com>; Schmidt, Lindsay <Lindsay_Schmidt@PremierInc.com>; Bossemeyer, Matthew <Matt_Bossemeyer@PremierInc.com>; Shimshock, Matthew <Matt_Shimshock@PremierInc.com>; Marquardt, Bill <Bill_Marquardt@PremierInc.com>; Juliano, Denise <Denise_Juliano@premierinc.com>; House, John <John_House@PremierInc.com>
Subject: Next Steps: Premier Data Contract 

 

Hi Andrew & Carlos,

 

Thank you for the continued dialogue over the past several months. We appreciate the time and thought both teams invested in exploring potential paths forward.

 

While we explored a number of options and concessions in good faith, we were ultimately unable to identify a viable structure that aligned with the value of the broader enterprise data bundle or our long-term commercialization strategy. As a result, our leadership has confirmed that we’re not able to move forward with an extension or restructuring of the proposal we offered to you.

 

The existing contract will expire as scheduled on March 30, 2026. The final Market Insights data delivery will be the last week of March. No additional data will be provided beyond that contractual term. Market Insights as a product is being sunset as a standalone offering in this channel, which further limits our ability to revisit this engagement in its current form.

 

I have valued the collaboration with you both since joining the discussions. While this particular product and structure are concluding, we remain open to future conversations should there be alignment with Clarivate’s evolving data strategy and business needs. 

 

Best regards,

Kari

bodyText:
Team,

 

GE is requesting a pause to our non-GPO related selling activities including market insights, managed accounts receivable, applied sciences data, brain collab and inventory data due to personnel moves and ramp up for Charity.  We will have an enterprise strategy session in CLT later this Spring and I will coordinate with everyone to provide updates on opportunities.  Thank you.

 

Regards,

 

Pete

Subject: Introducing John Little

 

Please join me in welcoming John Little to Premier.  John will be supporting the AllSpire Members as Vice President Account Executive.  Reporting to John will be Amanda Whiffing, Jennifer Taylor, and Angela Burkholder.  John’s experience will allow us to improve our overall partnership with AllSpire and its Members.  

 

John is a seasoned healthcare executive with more than 30 years of leadership experience in supply chain, procurement, contracting, and GPO engagement. Before joining Premier, he served in a senior leadership role at Jubilant Radiopharma, a national provider of radiopharmaceuticals and nuclear medicine services. Throughout his career, he has consistently driven value creation, strengthened supply and contract performance, and built effective partnerships with health systems, GPOs, and industry stakeholders.

 

He previously held executive roles including Executive Vice President at CoalesCo, Inc., where he led two regional GPOs—Cooperative Services of Florida, Inc. and SCM Alliance, Inc.—overseeing operations, contracting strategy, and portfolio development across key healthcare categories. Prior to CoalesCo, he served as Senior Director of Supply Chain at Vizient. His expertise includes developing high‑performing teams, aligning financial and clinical priorities, and advancing innovative supply chain strategies.

 

John lives in Buffalo, NY; his wife is an executive chef and has two children in college. He is an avid outdoors enthusiast who enjoys camping, hiking, and golf. He is also a committed fan of the Ole Miss Rebels and the Buffalo Bills. Outside of work, he mentors emerging supply chain professionals and remains active in healthcare leadership initiatives.

 

Welcome John!

 

Kevin

 

Kevin DeBruyne

East Zone General Manager

Premier, Inc.
